compiled_state.title: Brainstorm approval gate: non-approving option selected with 'Approval' header -> skill proceeds to Phase 3 without explicit approval
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description
The brainstorm skill's Phase 2 Step 4 approval gate (presented via AskUserQuestion) proceeded to Phase 3 ticket creation after the user selected a non-approving option. The user selected 'Discuss more' at the approval gate, engaged in further discussion, and eventually approved — but the skill did not re-present the gate cleanly before proceeding.

## User Hypothesis
The AskUserQuestion call uses 'Approval' as the header/chip label for the question. Because the header is labeled 'Approval', any response — including 'Discuss more', 'Re-run scenarios', or other non-approving options — may be interpreted in the session log or by the orchestrating agent as an affirmative approval signal. The agent reads the question header ('Approval') rather than the selected option value when determining whether to proceed.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run /dso:brainstorm on an epic through full scrutiny pipeline
2. At the Phase 2 Step 4 approval gate, select any non-approving option (e.g., 'Discuss more')
3. Observe whether the skill correctly pauses and engages in discussion, or incorrectly proceeds to Phase 3 (ticket creation/editing)

## Expected Behavior
Selecting any option other than 'Approve' at the approval gate should pause skill execution. Phase 3 should only begin after the user explicitly selects the 'Approve' option in a fresh re-presentation of the gate.

## Actual Behavior
Skill proceeded toward Phase 3 actions (ticket edit, follow-on epic creation) after a non-approving gate response, with the discussion loop not cleanly separating from the proceed signal.

## Hypothesis
The AskUserQuestion 'header' field (displayed as a chip/tag, max 12 chars) is set to 'Approval' — this label may be interpreted by the agent as the answer value rather than the question label when parsing the AskUserQuestion response in context. Suggested fix: rename the header to something neutral like 'Gate' or 'Action', and ensure the skill explicitly checks that the answer value equals the 'Approve' option label before proceeding to Phase 3.

## Affected File
plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md — Phase 2 Step 4 Approval Gate section

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
